campus: Rome (Department of Computer, Control and Management Engineering 'Antonio Ruberti' - DIAG)
faculty: Faculty of Information Engineering, Informatics and Statistics (Department DIAG)
admission_test_type: Academic knowledge assessment (curricular requirement check + evaluation of academic profile, possibly an interview); no written entrance test
application_platform: MoveIN platform (sapienza.gomovein.com) for non-EU pre-selection; Infostud for admission/enrolment
access_quota_non_eu: Maximum 25 applicants per individual non-EU country (general Sapienza MoveIN rule); programme-specific non-EU contingent per the call [uncertain]
places_and_foreign_contingent: Programme places and non-EU contingent set in the official call; exact 2026-2027 figures not extracted [uncertain]

academic_requirements.prior_degree: A three-year bachelor's degree (Laurea) or equivalent foreign first-level degree.
academic_requirements.curricular_requirements: More than 96 ECTS/CFU validated in the specified foundational and core Scientific-Disciplinary Sectors (SSD) of mathematics, physics, informatics and information engineering.
academic_requirements.personal_preparation: Academic knowledge assessment by a Committee considering bachelor's degree average grade, curriculum pertinence, extracurricular activities, and possibly an interview.

country_specific_eligibility: Foreign first-level degrees must be recognized as comparable; per Sapienza/MoveIN academic requirements 2026-2027. Specific country rules not individually extracted [uncertain]
curricular_integration_policy: If curricular ECTS thresholds are not met in the required SSDs, admission is not granted (curricular gaps must be filled before the master, per programme regulations) [uncertain]
conditional_enrolment_policy: Applicants in the final year of their bachelor's may apply, with enrolment conditional on completing the degree by the enrolment deadline [uncertain]

language_requirements.note: Students must have a good command of written and spoken English equivalent to at least B2; the programme is taught entirely in English.

language_submission_timing: At MoveIN pre-selection (non-EU) and/or by enrolment; exact timing per the call [uncertain]
italian_language_requirement: Italian not required for admission; programme fully in English.
application_deadline_eu: Open until 31 July 2026 for EU and EU-equivalent candidates (A.Y. 2026-2027). [uncertain - programme-specific internal admission deadlines may differ]
application_deadline_non_eu: MoveIN pre-selection window 22 December 2025 - 15 May 2026 for non-EU candidates residing abroad requiring a visa (A.Y. 2026-2027).
application_fee: €30 per application via MoveIN pre-selection (max 2 applications per candidate per intake)
visa_preenrolment_deadline: MoveIN pre-selection by 15 May 2026; subsequent visa pre-enrolment via Universitaly per national deadline
required_documents: Academic transcript with ECTS/CFU and SSD detail
English language certificate (B2) or evidence of English credits
For foreign qualifications: Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA statement, legalized/translated documents
document_translation_authentication: Foreign documents require official translation and legalization/apostille, plus a Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA comparability statement.

entry_exam_or_test.selection: Verification of curricular requirements (>96 ECTS in required SSDs) followed by an academic knowledge assessment / evaluation of academic profile, possibly including an interview.
entry_exam_or_test.evaluation_factors: Bachelor's degree average grade, curriculum pertinence, extracurricular activities, possible interview.

test_variant_and_session: Evaluation conducted by the Area Educational Board Committee; sessions per the faculty calendar [uncertain]
test_score_conversion_or_threshold: Admission by meeting curricular thresholds and positive personal-preparation assessment; no numeric public threshold [uncertain]

source_quotes.quote: Admission requires having over 96 ECTS credits in foundational and core subjects within certain Scientific-Disciplinary Sectors, a minimum English language proficiency level of B2, and a successful academic knowledge assessment.
Admission to the Master's Degree in Artificial Intelligence and Robotics is subject to an academic knowledge assessment, whose procedures are laid down in the Programme Regulations.
A maximum of 25 applicants per individual non-EU country will be admitted.
All pre-selection candidates are required to pay a €30 application fee for each submitted application (up to a maximum of two applications per candidate per intake).

uncertainty_notes: Programme-specific places and non-EU contingent for 2026-2027 not extracted from the official call; only the general MoveIN rule (max 25 per non-EU country) confirmed.
official_call_url and faculty_call_pdf_url not retrieved; should be located on corsidilaurea documenti_ufficiali / DIAG.
Curricular ECTS '>96' and required SSD list summarized from the catalogue; exact SSD codes per the Programme Regulations not transcribed.
EU programme-internal admission deadline vs. the general 31 July 2026 enrolment deadline may differ for restricted-access masters; confirm on the call.
